audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for El Shaddai, walk before me, tamim, covenant multiplication, Abram/Abraham and Sarai/Sarah renaming, circumcision sign, Isaac naming, and Ishmael blessing. Final source-critical review may refine divine title nuance.

verses.restored: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the Eternal appeared to Abram and said to him, I am El Shaddai. Walk before me and be complete.

familiar.text: And when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the LORD appeared to Abram, and said unto him, I am the Almighty God...

notes.title: El Shaddai
notes.body: El Shaddai is traditionally rendered God Almighty. The exact nuance is debated, so the divine title is preserved.

notes.title: Be complete
notes.body: Tamim means complete, whole, blameless, or sound.

verses.restored: I will give my covenant between me and you, and I will multiply you very, very greatly.

familiar.text: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.

notes.title: Very, very greatly
notes.body: The Hebrew intensifies multiplication with meod meod.

verses.restored: Abram fell on his face, and the Divine spoke with him, saying,

familiar.text: And Abram fell on his face: and God talked with him, saying,

verses.restored: As for me, look, my covenant is with you, and you shall become father of a multitude of nations.

familiar.text: As for me, behold, my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.

verses.restored: Your name shall no longer be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham, for I have made you father of a multitude of nations.

familiar.text: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham...

notes.title: Abraham
notes.body: The renaming ties Abraham to a multitude of nations. The exact etymology is interpretive, but the text explains it through hamon, multitude.

verses.restored: I will make you very, very fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come out from you.

familiar.text: And I will make thee exceeding fruitful, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.

verses.restored: I will establish my covenant between me and you and your seed after you throughout their generations, as an everlasting covenant, to be Divine to you and to your seed after you.

familiar.text: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee...

notes.title: Everlasting covenant
notes.body: Berit olam marks an enduring covenant across generations.

verses.restored: I will give to you and to your seed after you the land of your sojournings, all the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession, and I will be Divine to them.

familiar.text: And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger...

verses.restored: The Divine said to Abraham, As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your seed after you throughout their generations.

familiar.text: And God said unto Abraham, Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore...

verses.restored: This is my covenant, which you shall keep between me and you and your seed after you: every male among you shall be circumcised.

familiar.text: This is my covenant, which ye shall keep... Every man child among you shall be circumcised.

notes.title: Circumcision sign
notes.body: The covenant sign is embodied in male flesh. This is a sign of belonging in the Abrahamic household.

verses.restored: You shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.

familiar.text: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant...

verses.restored: At eight days old, every male among you shall be circumcised throughout your generations, one born in the house or bought with silver from any foreigner who is not of your seed.

familiar.text: And he that is eight days old shall be circumcised among you...

notes.title: Household covenant
notes.body: The sign includes household-born and purchased males, reflecting ancient household structures including slavery.

verses.restored: He who is born in your house and he who is bought with your silver must surely be circumcised. My covenant shall be in your flesh as an everlasting covenant.

familiar.text: He that is born in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised...

verses.restored: The uncircumcised male, who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin, that person shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.

familiar.text: And the uncircumcised man child... that soul shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.

notes.title: Cut off
notes.body: Karat, cut off, plays against the cutting of the covenant sign. Refusal of the sign breaks covenant belonging.

verses.restored: The Divine said to Abraham, As for Sarai your woman, you shall not call her name Sarai, for Sarah is her name.

familiar.text: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah...

notes.title: Sarah named
notes.body: Sarai is renamed Sarah alongside Abraham’s renaming, marking her direct inclusion in the covenant promise.

verses.restored: I will bless her, and also from her I will give you a son. I will bless her, and she shall become nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.

familiar.text: And I will bless her, and give thee a son also of her... kings of people shall be of her.

notes.title: From her
notes.body: Sarah herself is the promised mother, not a passive container. Nations and kings are promised from her.

verses.restored: Abraham fell on his face and laughed. He said in his heart, Shall a child be born to one who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear?

familiar.text: Then Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart...

notes.title: Laughed
notes.body: Abraham’s laughter prepares for Isaac’s name, Yitzchaq, linked with laughter.

verses.restored: Abraham said to the Divine, If only Ishmael might live before you!

familiar.text: And Abraham said unto God, O that Ishmael might live before thee!

notes.title: Ishmael before God
notes.body: Abraham’s concern for Ishmael is answered with blessing, though covenant line is distinguished through Isaac.

verses.restored: The Divine said, No, Sarah your woman shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his seed after him.

familiar.text: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall bear thee a son indeed; and thou shalt call his name Isaac...

notes.title: Isaac
notes.body: Isaac, Yitzchaq, is linked with laughter. The covenant line is established through Sarah’s son.

verses.restored: As for Ishmael, I have heard you. Look, I have blessed him, and I will make him fruitful and multiply him very, very greatly. He shall father twelve princes, and I will make him into a great nation.

familiar.text: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee: Behold, I have blessed him...

notes.title: I have heard you
notes.body: Ishmael’s name means God hears, and the Divine again hears concerning him.

notes.title: Great nation
notes.body: Ishmael receives real blessing and nationhood, not rejection from divine care.

verses.restored: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this appointed time next year.

familiar.text: But my covenant will I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall bear unto thee at this set time...

notes.title: Appointed time
notes.body: Moed marks the specific appointed time for Isaac’s birth.

verses.restored: When he finished speaking with him, the Divine went up from Abraham.

familiar.text: And he left off talking with him, and God went up from Abraham.

verses.restored: Abraham took Ishmael his son and all those born in his house and all those bought with his silver, every male among the men of Abraham’s house, and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin that very day, as the Divine had spoken with him.

familiar.text: And Abraham took Ishmael his son, and all that were born in his house...

notes.title: That very day
notes.body: Abraham immediately enacts the covenant sign across his household.

verses.restored: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

familiar.text: And Abraham was ninety years old and nine, when he was circumcised...

verses.restored: Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

familiar.text: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old, when he was circumcised...

verses.restored: On that very day Abraham was circumcised, and Ishmael his son.

familiar.text: In the selfsame day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his son.

verses.restored: All the men of his house, those born in the house and those bought with silver from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.

familiar.text: And all the men of his house, born in the house, and bought with money of the stranger...
